title: Set Up Cloudflare Pages for React Frontend Deployment
description:
Set up Cloudflare Pages for the React v3 frontend deployment with proper security configuration.

### A) Cloudflare Pages for React
- Project `dtp-app-v3` connected to Git repository
- Domain: `us1.datatoolspro.com`
- SPA fallback configured for deep link refresh support
- Build options: Direct Git (auto-deploy on push) or GitHub Actions (wrangler pages deploy)
- Environment variable: `VITE_API_URL` set per environment

### B) Cloudflare Proxied API and Origin Lockdown
For `us1api.datatoolspro.com`:
- Orange-cloud DNS record (proxied through Cloudflare)
- WAF managed rules ON
- Bot protection ON
- Rate limiting: strict on `/auth/*`, moderate on `/api/*`
- Origin lockdown: Cloudflare IP ranges only, or Authenticated Origin Pulls (mTLS)

### C) CORS and Cookies
- Laravel CORS allowlist: `https://app.datatoolspro.com`, `https://staging.datatoolspro.com`
- Cookie settings: `Domain=.datatoolspro.com`, `Secure`, `HttpOnly`
- `SameSite=Lax` preferred; `None` only if required with tightened CSRF checks
- Never use `*` origin with credential cookies

### Security Model
Public pages (`/`, `/login`) are React routes. The app is gated because all meaningful actions require API calls, and the API enforces session, tenant, and RBAC. JavaScript bundles being publicly downloadable is acceptable — IP is in data and authorization logic, not the JS bundle.
acceptance_criteria: Cloudflare Pages project dtp-app-v3 is created and connected to Git
SPA fallback is configured for deep link refresh
API is proxied through Cloudflare with WAF, bot protection, and rate limiting
Origin lockdown prevents direct API access bypassing Cloudflare
CORS and cookie configuration supports cross-subdomain auth securely
docs/architecture/backend.md is updated with configuration decisions
middleware_changes: Laravel CORS allowlist updated. Cookie domain and SameSite configuration. CSRF/origin check tightening.
api_changes: Rate limiting rules for /auth/* (strict) and /api/* (moderate). Cloudflare WAF and bot protection on API endpoints.
implementation_notes: Two deployment options: Direct Git (Cloudflare auto-builds on push to main) or GitHub Actions (wrangler pages deploy via deploy-staging.yml / deploy-production.yml with approval). Origin lockdown options: firewall to Cloudflare IPs, Authenticated Origin Pulls (TLS client cert), or mTLS. Must choose and enforce one to prevent attackers from hitting the API server directly.
